info.title: PowerFlex Block Storage REST API
info.description: Describes the API calls for PowerFlex block storage.

get.description: Retrieves a list of system objects.

Retrieves a list of protection domains in the specified system.
Retrieves a list of SDC objects in the specified system.

Retrieves a list of snapshot policy objects in the specified system.

post.description: Create an MDM cluster.

name.description: Name of the created MDM. Each MDM name must be unique. Ensure that each server on which the MDM is installed has a unique hostname. NOTE: If the error message SERVER_CERTIFICATE_NOT_TRUSTED is returned, the MDM's host name is not a unique name. To solve the problem, change the MDM host's name.

updateConfiguration.description: If the value is set to TRUE and the mdmAddresses parameter in the REST gateway configuration is empty, the mdmAddresses will be populated with the values from the IP addresses.

post.description:
Add a standby MDM to the system.

Note: The addStandbyMdm request does not add the IP addresses to the mdmAddresses field. Instead, post an /api/updateConfiguration request to manually update the IP addresses.

post.description: Reset the specified SDC oscillating counters to zero. This command is useful when you have fixed a problem and want to ensure that an alert is no longer active in the system.

FeatureInfoList is a list of objects with two properties: featureCode and expirationDays

post.description:
Creates a thin copy of a storage object. 

A thin clone is a read/write copy of a volume or a snapshot that allows you to change its content through writes.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id is the system id.

snapshotDefs.description:
The snapshotDef accepts the folowing properties: 
- volumeId (required) - the id of the volume(s) to clone.
- snapshotName (optional) - the name(s) of the clones created.
- volumeClass (optional) - the class of the volume.

post.description:
Creates a snapshot of a storage volume. 

A snapshot is a read-only copy of the volume that provides local data protection and enables recovery in case of corruption or accidental deletion.

parameters.name: id

snapshotDefs.description:
The snapshotDef accepts the folowing properties: 
- volumeId (required) - the id of the volume(s) to snapshot.
- snapshotName (optional) - the name(s) of the snapshots created.
- volumeClass (optional) - the class of the volume.

retentionPeriodInMin.description: The retention periods marks the snapshot as secured and specifies the retention length. For example, 1000.

get.description: Retrieves a list of devices in the system.

post.description: Add a storage device.

parameters.name: deviceCurrentPathname
parameters.description: Full path of the device to be added. A device can be a disk, an unmounted partition, or a file that represents free space on a mounted device.

parameters.name: longSuccessfulIos
parameters.description: A troubleshooting counter for I/Os to disk that took longer than expected, but still ended successfully. This can be used to identify malfunctioning disks.

parameters.name: testTimeSecs
parameters.description: The maximal test run-time in seconds or 128 MB data. Not relevant for noTest.

parameters.name: testMode
parameters.description:
One of:
testOnly - Devices will be tested but not used. Later issue activateDevice to start using their capacity.
noTest - Device's capacity will be used without any device testing.
testAndActivate - Devices will be tested and activated.
NOTE:	If testMode is not part of the request body, read and write tests will be run on devices before their capacity is used.

parameters.name: forceDeviceTakeover
parameters.description:
Force adding the device.
WARNING:	Use this flag with caution, because all data on the device will be destroyed.
During execution of the command, PowerFlex checks that the device is clear before adding it. If the device is not clear, an error message is returned, and the command fails for that device. If you would like to overwrite existing data on the device by forcing the command, use the forceDeviceTakeover flag.

errorState.description:
Indicates the reason that a device is in an error state:
None—the device is fine
Error—there is a problem with the device that is causing I/Os to fail
Warning
Notice
Info
Acceleration— the device is fine, but the acceleration device it relies on is in error, so the device is unusable
Unrecoverable— the device is a fine granularity device and a checksum error was detected on it, or it's a fine granularity device which is not of type SSD

SDC.title: SDC
SDC.description:
From Powerflex 4.0, a storage data client (SDC) object also represents NVMe host. This can be determined by examining the value of the hostType property. When an SDC object represents an NVMe host, all relationship URLs will return host URIs.
Host URIs are also available for getting all instance, specific instance, and statistic. Host URIs behaves same as the equivalent SDC URI. For example, instance returnS a list of all hosts (both NVMe and SDC).

avg_host_write_latency.description: A storage performance metric that measures the average time it takes for a host to complete write operations to storage.

host_trim_bandwidth.description: A storage performance metric that measures the data throughput (bandwidth) associated with TRIM operations initiated by the host.

host_trim_iops.description: A storage performance metric measuring the number of TRIM operations per second initiated by the host.

protection_domain_gen_type.description:
A protection domain generation type refers to the method used to create and organize protection domains.
The choices are: Erasure coding (EC) or mirroring.

avg_host_read_latency.description: A storage performance metric that measures the average time it takes for a host to complete read operations from storage.

logical_provisioned.description: The total amount of virtual or logical storage capacity allocated to workloads, regardless of how much physical space is actually consumed.

host_read_bandwidth.description: A storage performance metric that measures the rate at which data is read from storage by the host.

host_read_iops.description: A storage performance metric that measures the number of read input/output operations per second initiated by the host.

logical_used.description: A storage capacity metric that represents the amount of logical (virtual) storage space actually consumed by data, including any overhead from snapshots and thin clones, but before physical efficiency features like compression are applied.

host_write_bandwidth.description: A storage performance metric that measures the rate at which data is written from the host to the storage system.

host_write_iops.description: A storage performance metric that measures the number of write input/output operations per second initiated by the host.

avg_host_trim_latency.description: A storage performance metric that measures the average time it takes for the host to complete TRIM operations on a storage device.

childVolumeIds.description: A list of identifiers that reference subordinate or nested volume objects within a parent volume.

numOfChildVolumes.description: The total number of child volumes associated with a parent volume object.

numOfDescendantVolumes.description: The total number of volume objects that are hierarchically nested under the current volume, including both direct children and all deeper nested descendants.

numOfMappedSdcs.description: Indicates the number of storage data clients (SDCs) that are mapped to the volume object.

registrationKey.description: A unique identifier or token used to authenticate, activate, or associate a volume object with a specific system.

idevice_local_read_bandwidthd.description: A value representing the maximum rate at which data can be read from device-local memory.

device_local_read_iops.description: A value representing the number of read input/output operations per second that can be performed on device-local memory.

avg_host_read_latency.description: A value representing the average time it takes to complete a read operation from host memory to the processing unit.

raw_used.description: A value representing the amount of raw storage currently in use.

logical_used.description: A value representing the amount of logical storage in use.

host_write_bandwidth.description: A value representing the maximum rate at which data can be written from the host system to a target device or storage.

host_write_iops.description: A value representing the number of write input/output operations per second that the host system (e.g., CPU or main memory) can perform when writing data to a device or storage.

avg_device_pmem_write_latency.description: A value representing the average time required to complete a write operation to persistent memory (PMem) on the device.

device_remote_read_bandwidth.description: A value representing the maximum rate at which data can be read from remote device memory to the local system.

device_remote_read_iops.description: A value representing the number of read input/output operations per second that can be performed when accessing remote device memory over a network.

storage_fe_write_bandwidth.description: A value representing the maximum rate at which data can be written to the front-end of the storage system.

storage_fe_write_iops.description: A number representing the number of write input/output operations per second that can be performed through the front-end interface of the storage system.

avg_fe_write_io_size.description: A value representing the average size, in bytes, of write input/output operations performed through the front-end interface of the storage system.

raw_free.description: A value representing the free raw capacity that is available to allocate in the system.

storage_fe_read_bandwidth.description: A value representing the maximum rate at which data can be read from the front-end of the storage system to the host.

storage_fe_read_iops.description: A value representing the maximum rate at which data can be read from the front-end of the storage system to the host.

avg_fe_read_io_size.description: Measures the average size of read operations initiated by the front-end.

host_read_bandwidth.description: Measures the amount of data that is read by the host from the storage system over a given time interval.

host_read_iops.description: Quantifies the number of read requests issued by the host to the storage system each second.

total_device_pmem_write_bandwidth.description: Measures the aggregate rate at which data is being written to th persistent memory devices.

total_device_pmem_write_iops.description: Measures the aggregate write IOPS across the persistent memory devices.

avg_device_pmem_write_io_size.description: Measures the average size of write input/output operations performed on persistent memory devices.

data_reduction_ratio.description: The ratio describes the logical used space to the data physical space after data reduction, for example, compression.

thin_provisioning_ratio.description: The ratio describes the extent to which storage is over-allocated compared to the actual physical capacity available.

total_device_pmem_read_bandwidth.description: The total rate at which data is read from persistent memory devices in a system.

total_device_pmem_read_iops.description: Measures the total number of read I/O operations per second (IOPS) performed on the persistent memory devices in the system.

avg_device_pmem_read_io_size.description: Measures the average size (in bytes) of read I/O operations performed on persistent memory devices.

total_device_read_bandwidth.description: Measures the aggregate read bandwidth across all devices, i.e., the total amount of data read per second.

total_device_read_iops.description: Measures the total number of read I/O operations per second (IOPS) across the devices in the system.

avg_device_read_io_size.description: Measures the average size of read I/O operations performed on the devices during the measurement interval.

storage_fe_read_latency.description: Measures the average time taken to complete a read I/O request from the host perspective, including queuing and processing at the front-end.

raw_total.description: The total sum of devices in the system.

unreducible_data.description: Measures the amount of data that cannot be reduced.

patterns_saving_ratio.description: Measures the percentage of space saved by identifying and eliminating repetitive patterns in the data.

avg_host_write_latency.description: Measures the average time taken to complete a write I/O request from the host to the storage system.

device_remote_write_bandwidth.description: Measures the amount of data written per second to remote devices or nodes over the network.

device_remote_write_iops.description: Measures the number of remote write I/O operations per second (IOPS) sent from the local device to remote devices or nodes.

storage_fe_write_latency.description: Measures the average time taken to complete a write I/O request from the host.

total_device_write_iops.description: Measures the total number of write I/O operations per second (IOPS) across all devices in the system.

avg_device_write_io_size.description: The average size of write I/O operations performed on the devices during the measurement interval.

logical_provisioned.description: Represents the total logical capacity allocated to hosts or applications, regardless of the actual physical space consumed.

efficiency_ratio.description: Measures the overall storage efficiency, expressed as a ratio of logical capacity (what hosts see) to physical capacity (what is actually consumed).

raw_system.description: The raw capacity that is in use by the system for internal use by the system.

storage_fe_trim_latency.description: The average time taken to process TRIM (or UNMAP) commands from the host at the front-end interface.

device_local_write_bandwidth.description: The amount of data written per second to local devices, excluding any remote or replicated writes.

device_local_write_iops.description: Measures the number of local write I/O operations per second (IOPS) executed on these devices, excluding any remote or replicated writes.

physical_system.description: The amount of storage that the user data and snapshots take after data reduction in the system. The physical resides on the storage pools.

avg_device_write_latency.description: Measures the average time taken to complete a write I/O operation on the device(s) during the measurement interval.

storage_fe_trim_bandwidth.description: Measures the rate at which TRIM (or UNMAP) commands are processed by the storage system at the front-end interface.

storage_fe_trim_iops.description: Measures the number of TRIM (or UNMAP) operations per second processed by the storage system at the front-end interface.

avg_fe_trim_io_size.description: Measures the average size of TRIM I/O operations handled at the front-end interface during the observation period.

compression_ratio.description: Measures the ratio of original (uncompressed) data size to compressed data size, indicating how much space savings compression achieved.

avg_device_pmem_read_latency.description: Measures the average time taken to complete read I/O operations on PMEM devices that belong to a specific volume group.

physical_used.description: The physical capacity used by user data in the system.

snapshot_saving_ratio.description: Measures how effective snapshots are in reducing storage consumption. A higher ratio means more space savings because snapshots reuse existing blocks instead of creating full copies.

physical_free.description: The amount of physical storage space currently available (unused) on the system, after accounting for: Data stored, system overhead and data reduction effects.

host_trim_bandwidth.description: A configuration that limits or reduces the bandwidth available to a specific host on a network.

host_trim_iops.description: The number of I/O operations per second associated with TRIM commands issued by a host to a storage device.

physical_total.description: The total amount of physical memory (RAM) installed in a system.

avg_device_read_latency.description: The average time it takes for a storage device to complete read operations.

avg_host_trim_latency.description: The average time it takes for a host system to complete TRIM operations on a storage device.

reducible_ratio.description: The proportion of data that can be reduced relative to the total data stored.

logical_owned.description: The total logical capacity assigned to a host.

logical_provisioned.description: The logical capacity in use by user data and snapshots in the system.

logical_used.description: The capacity used by user data in the system.

thin_provisioning_ration.description: The ratio between provisioned and logical used capacity.

patterns_saving_ratio.description: Indicates the efficiency of data reduction achieved by applying specific storage or compression patterns.

snapshot_saving_ratio.description: Measures the storage efficiency achieved by using snapshots compared to the original data size.

efficiency_ratio.description: Measures how effectively data reduction techniques reduce physical storage usage compared to logical data size.

logical_owned.description: The logical capacity that is owned or allocated to a volume within a storage system.

resourceType.description: Discriminator selecting which resource metrics schema applies.

numOfProtectionDomains.description: A list of protection domain IDs.

numOfDgwt.description: The number of device gateway targets.

numOfLibsdc.description: The number of library SDCs present in the system.

numDgsRebuildInProgress.description: The number of device groups that are undergoing a rebuild operation.

numDevErrors.description: The number of device errors in the system.

numSmartAttributesPassedThreshold.description: The number of smart attributes that have passed a certain threshold. A smart attribute relatees to the health and performance of devices in the system.

numOfUserVolumes.description: The number of volumes that are used by users

numOfMappedToAllVolumes.description: The number of volumes that are currently exposed to all SDCs or NVMe nodes in the system.

numOfUnmappedVolumes.description: The number of volumes that are not currently mapped to any SDCs or NVMe nodes.

numOfVolumesInDeletion.description: The number of volumes that are being deleted.

snapPolicyIds.description: A list of snapshot policy IDs.

numPdsSdcDisconnections.description: The number of disconnections between protection domains and SDCs.

numPdsSdtDisconnections.description: The number of protection domain to SDT disconnections.

hostGroupIds.description: A list of host group IDs.

avg_host_to_write_latency.description: The average time it takes for a request or message to travel from a host to the controller.

host_trim_bandwidth.description: The average time it takes for a response or command to travel from the controller to the host.

avg_host_read_latency.description: The average time it takes for a host system to complete read operations from a storage device or system.

sessionTag.description: A session where the system communicates with the MDM about the delta. The same delta is echoed back in the next call within that session. Use 0 for the first call.

lastSnapshotPolicyVersion.description: The session identifier for the next call; use 0 for the first call.

isDirty.description: Indicates that data changed during request processing; changes might not appear in the response. Consider an extra query to check for missed updates.

snapshotPolicyIsPartial.description: ndicates that only partial data was returned because the response exceeded the MDM size limit; fetch the remaining data with another query using sessionTag and lastSnapshotPolicyVersion.

deletedSnapshotPolicyIdList.description: List of IDs for SnapshotPolicies deleted since the last query. If null, the response is full (not delta), so any SnapshotPolicies not included should be considered deleted. When combined with snapshotPolicyIsPartial, deletions are confirmed only after a non-partial response. Account for delete-and-recreate bursts if relevant.

sessionTag.description: Session for communicating delta changes with MDM; echoed back in the next call. Use 0 for the first call.

lastStoragePoolVersion.description: Identifier for the next call within the session; use 0 for the first call.

isDirty.description: Indicates data changed during request processing; changes may not appear in the response, so an extra query might be needed to catch missed updates.

storagePoolIsPartial.description: Indicates only partial data was returned because the response exceeded the MDM size limit; remaining data must be fetched with another query using sessionTag and lastStoragePoolVersion.

storagePoolList.description: List of StoragePool objects added or modified since the last query. If the response is full (deletedStoragePoolIdList = null), it includes all existing StoragePools—unless storagePoolIsPartial = true, in which case it’s only a subset.
